subtitle: A one-mile horseshoe of white sand and turquoise water on Culebra that islanders took back from the US Navy in 1975, where two rusting Sherman tanks still sit on the sand.
spike_statement: A US Navy live-fire bombing range until 1975, now ranked among the world's best beaches, with two graffiti-covered Sherman tanks still on the sand.

levers.photo_tone: turquoise horseshoe bay and bright white sand under Caribbean light, with the oxidized rust and layered graffiti of an M4 Sherman tank at the western tree line
